verses.text: Hananiah prophesies that within two years God will break Babylon's yoke and restore the vessels and captives. He contradicts Jeremiah. False prophets promise quick relief to please the people.
Hananiah prophesies that within two years God will break Babylon's yoke and restore the vessels and captives. He contradicts Jeremiah. False prophets promise quick relief to please the people.
Hananiah prophesies that within two years God will break Babylon's yoke and restore the vessels and captives. He contradicts Jeremiah. False prophets promise quick relief to please the people.
Hananiah prophesies that within two years God will break Babylon's yoke and restore the vessels and captives. He contradicts Jeremiah. False prophets promise quick relief to please the people.

Jeremiah answers, 'Amen: the LORD do so,' yet warns that a prophet of peace is proved only 'when the word... shall come to pass.' He longs for it to be true. But the test of a prophet is fulfilment, not pleasant words.

Jeremiah answers, 'Amen: the LORD do so,' yet warns that a prophet of peace is proved only 'when the word... shall come to pass.' He longs for it to be true. But the test of a prophet is fulfilment, not pleasant words.
Hananiah breaks the wooden yoke from Jeremiah's neck, declaring God will so break Babylon's yoke. A bold pretence. False confidence acts dramatically to win the crowd.
Hananiah breaks the wooden yoke from Jeremiah's neck, declaring God will so break Babylon's yoke. A bold pretence. False confidence acts dramatically to win the crowd.
God answers that Hananiah has broken yokes of wood, but God will make 'yokes of iron'; Babylon's rule will be the firmer. Defiance hardens the burden. To resist God's decree only makes it heavier.

God answers that Hananiah has broken yokes of wood, but God will make 'yokes of iron'; Babylon's rule will be the firmer. Defiance hardens the burden. To resist God's decree only makes it heavier.

Jeremiah tells Hananiah he makes the people 'trust in a lie,' and this year he shall die; and Hananiah died in the seventh month. The false prophet is judged. God vindicates His true word by the lying prophet's fall.
